vulnerability.reference: Users lack technical knowledge to scrutinize plausible-seeming but false explanations.
vulnerability.generated: Consumers and patients lack the technical literacy and independent verification mechanisms to detect manipulated explanations presented as authoritative by AI interfaces.

impact.reference: Wrongly denied services go uncontested, causing individual harm and deepening power asymmetries.
impact.generated: Loss of ability to contest decisions entrenches erroneous AI outcomes, causing uncorrected harms and undermining accountability.

dose_response.reference: Multiplicity of XAI methods enables plausible but incorrect explanations, which when presented to users lacking technical knowledge leads to uncontested wrongful denials, causing harm and power asymmetries.
dose_response.generated: Multiplicity of XAI methods enabling X-hacking, through consumers and patients receiving AI-powered recommendations, triggers their lack of technical literacy to detect manipulated explanations, leading to loss of ability to contest decisions, uncorrected harms, and undermined accountability.
